questions.question: What is the purpose of a database management system?
options: A) To manage a single database file
B) To manage multiple databases
C) To manage data and provide access control
D) To manage data only and not provide access control
questions.explanation: A database management system (DBMS) is designed to manage data and provide access control to authorized users.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of a relational database management system?
options: A) Ability to store large amounts of data
B) Ability to provide data redundancy
C) Ability to manage multiple data types
D) Ability to enforce data relationships
questions.explanation: A relational database management system is characterized by its ability to enforce data relationships between different data types.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the database schema?
options: A) To define the physical storage structure of the database
B) To define the logical structure of the database
C) To define the data types and relationships between different data entities
D) To define the user interface of the database
questions.explanation: The database schema defines the logical structure of the database, including the relationships between different data entities.

questions.question: What is the purpose of data normalization?
options: A) To reduce data redundancy
B) To increase data storage capacity
C) To improve data security
D) To reduce data processing time
questions.explanation: Data normalization is the process of organizing data to reduce data redundancy and improve data integrity.

questions.question: What is the term for a database that is not structured or organized?
options: A) Unstructured data
B) Semi-structured data
C) Structured data
D) Informal data
questions.explanation: Unstructured data refers to data that is not organized or structured in a specific format.
